name: What should I look for when choosing a flooring installer so I don’t get a bad job?
headline: What should I look for when choosing a flooring installer so I don’t get a bad job?

mainEntity.name: What should I look for when choosing a flooring installer so I don’t get a bad job?
mainEntity.text: What should I look for when choosing a flooring installer so I don’t get a bad job?

acceptedAnswer.text: When hiring a flooring installer, look beyond price and check for communication, experience, and attention to detail. A good installer should show up on time for the estimate, listen to your needs, and clearly explain the process. Poor communication early on can be a red flag. Ask how they handle subfloor preparation; almost every floor needs some correcting, and skipping this step can cause long-term problems. Request photos of past projects similar to yours and ask for references. Online reviews can help, but not everyone posts their experiences, good or bad. During your meeting, see if they talk about leveling, moisture testing, and following manufacturer instructions. If they do not mention these topics unless you ask, that may be a warning sign. Make sure you get a written estimate that lists demo, prep, materials, installation, trim, and any potential extras. Ask about warranties on both the materials and the labor. Check that they are properly licensed and insured for your area. Some warning signs include rushing the estimate, promising to start immediately without checking the subfloor, or refusing to explain their steps. A contractor who takes time to inspect your home and answer questions up front is more likely to deliver a quality job.
